text.title: FinOps — Rightsizing + Graviton Migration Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in production. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Workload sized at m5.large (p50 CPU 40%); p95 hits 95%; production latency p99 spikes 10x.
antipattern.root-cause: Author rightsizes to p50 utilization without checking the upper tail.
antipattern.fix: Resize to p95 + 20% headroom; verify latency p99 stays within SLO during the next peak; document the headroom rule.

antipattern.symptom: Recommended m5.xlarge over m6g.xlarge; team leaves ~25% savings on the table for a workload that runs Java/Python (Arm-compatible).
antipattern.root-cause: Author defaulted to x86 family without Graviton evaluation.
antipattern.fix: Re-evaluate with Graviton as the default; test under load on m7g/m6g; migrate when SLOs hold.

antipattern.symptom: Downsized to t3.medium; burst credits deplete during nightly batch; throttling event takes the service offline for 3 hours.
antipattern.fix: Switch back to a non-burstable family (m6g.medium) or T-instance with `unlimited` mode + cost cap; verify credits no longer deplete.
